body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 37/2026
Made under: Workers Compensation Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 16, 2026

B.C. Reg. 37/2026, deposited March 16, 2026, under theWORKERS COMPENSATION ACT[section 140]. Order in Council 80/2026, approved and ordered March 16, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that the Firefighters’ Occupational Disease Regulation, B.C. Reg. 125/2009, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — J. WHITESIDE,Minister of Labour; A. DIX,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 2 of the Firefighters’ Occupational Disease Regulation, B.C. Reg. 125/2009, is amended by adding the following paragraphs: (s)	primary site laryngeal cancer; (v)	primary site tracheal cancer; (w)	primary site bronchial cancer; (y)	primary site pharynx cancer. 2	The table in section 3 is amended (a)	in item 10 by striking out "20 years" and substituting "15 years", and (b)	by adding the following items as indicated: Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC adds 4 cancers to firefighters' occupational disease coverage, shortens service requirement
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has expanded the list of cancers that are automatically presumed to be work-related for firefighters under the Workers Compensation Act. Four new cancers — primary site laryngeal, tracheal, bronchial, and pharynx cancer — have been added to the Firefighters' Occupational Disease Regulation. The regulation also reduces the minimum years of service required for certain cancer claims from 20 years to 15 years. This means that if a firefighter develops one of these cancers and meets the service requirement, they do not have to prove the cancer was caused by their job — it is presumed to be work-related.

key_dates.label: BC firefighters' expanded cancer coverage takes effect
